session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8937 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by running the required commands to fetch and classify PR feedback for PR #8937.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: git diff --stat -M -C main...HEAD 2>/dev/null || git diff --stat -M -C origin/main...HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "No commits between branches"
Check for file restructuring in the PR

## ✅ Dignified Python Review

<summary>Details</summary>

✅ LBYL (Look Before You Leap) - No EAFP control flow patterns found
✅ Exception handling - Exceptions used only at boundaries
✅ Path operations - All path operations follow exists() before resolve() pattern
✅ Import organization - All imports module-level and absolute
✅ No default parameters - Function signatures compliant
✅ Frozen dataclasses - No dataclass violations

session.assistant:
Now I'll classify the PR feedback according to the skill's classification model:
The PR #8937 has no review threads (no code review comments), but has 6 discussion comments, all from bots and the submitter. Let me classify them:
1. **Comment ID 4017702650** (schrockn) - "Plan Queued for Implementation" - This is a metadata/workflow status comment, purely informational 2. **Comment ID 4017703402** (github-actions[bot]) - "GitHub Action Started" - Workflow status, purely informational 3. **Comment ID 4017705089** (github-actions[bot]) - "Starting implementation" - Workflow status, purely informational 4. **Comment ID 4017727263** (github-actions[bot]) - "Dignified Code Simplifier Review" - Status report (0 violations), informational 5. **Comment ID 4017727400** (github-actions[bot]) - "Dignified Python Review" - Status report (0 violations), informational 6. **Comment ID 4017728831** (github-actions[bot]) - "Tripwires Review" - Status report (0 violations), informational 7. **Comment ID 4017730287** (github-actions[bot]) - "Audit PR Docs" - **ACTIONABLE** - Found 3 violations (duplicative sections and drift risk) that need to be fixed
Based on the skill's classification rules: - Review threads: None - Actionable discussion comments: 1 (the audit-pr-docs with 3 violations) - Informational discussion comments: 6 (CI status updates and clean reviews)

Inline comments posted for each." } ], "informational_count": 6, "batches": [ { "name": "Pre-Existing (Auto-Resolve)", "complexity": "pre_existing", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Local Fixes", "complexity": "local", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Single-File", "complexity": "single_file", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [0] }, { "name": "Cross-Cutting", "complexity": "cross_cutting", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Complex", "complexity": "complex", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Informational", "complexity": "informational", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] } ], "error": null } ```
